title: citrus × bergamia var. vulgaris
intro: Citrus × bergamia var. vulgaris, also known as bergamot, is a variety of the citrus bergamia plant, which is a small, evergreen tree or large shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The essential oil extracted from the rind of bergamot fruits has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Bergamot has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving mood, and supporting cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Bergamot is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, essential oils, and aromatherapy products, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
